title: MARA Delivers Record Profits as Bitcoin Holdings Surge 197% to $4.6 Billion
text: Marathon Digital Holdings (NASDAQ: MARA) announced exceptional financial results for Q4 and full-year 2024, demonstrating significant growth despite industry headwinds. The Bitcoin miner reported full-year revenue growth of 69% to $656.4 million, with Q4 revenue increasing 37% to $214.4 million. Net income surged 248% to $528.3 million in Q4, while adjusted EBITDA reached $794.4 million, up 207% year-over-year. The company stated they accelerated their transition to a vertically integrated energy and digital infrastructure company. MARA's Bitcoin holdings expanded dramatically by 197% to 44,893 BTC, valued at approximately $4.6 billion at year-end. During Q4, the company mined 2,492 BTC and purchased an additional 15,574 BTC using convertible senior notes proceeds. The miner significantly expanded energy capacity, securing approximately 1.2 gigawatts at 28% lower costs than industry peers. This strategic move increased MARA's owned data center portfolio from 0% to approximately 70% since early 2024. MARA is positioning itself for the second wave of AI, focusing on edge inference rather than training. The company believes this strategic pause provides competitive advantages over competitors who rushed into AI hosting. Despite strong fundamentals, MARA stock currently trades near $12, its lowest level since November 2023—a 60% decline, reflecting the broader market correlation between mining stocks and Bitcoin price movements. Analyst consensus for 12-month price targets averages $26–27, with projections ranging from $20 (HC Wainwright) to $43 (Cantor Fitzgerald).
